Changed to present tense by adding تَ at start and changing vowels. The middle ا changes back to و in present tense.

Added ت at beginning to show 'you' and changed ending due to weak letter ي becoming ى in present tense
Added تَ at start to show 'you' and changed vowels for present tense. This is addressing Prophet Muhammad ﷺ in the ayah.

The ى changes back to يْ before adding suffixes

Changed from 'he gave' to 'he is given' by adding يُ at the start. The ؤ appears between يُ and ت. The ى at the end is dropped in this position.

Changed the root letters into a past tense verb by adding vowels, and turned و into ا since it's at the end of a past tense verb
Changed middle letter و to ا and added vowels to make 'he had ability' (past tense verb)

note: Added يُ at start to show present tense. The ي at the end drops because it's a weak letter in a jussive mood verb. The word is passive, showing someone receiving wisdom in this ayah.

Adding definite article and extending the final vowel with alif
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel to ُو
Changes the final و to ا to make it easier to say the word for interest/usury
Added vowels and the middle letter changed to آ (long aa sound). This shows the action happened in the past.
Added يُ at the start to make it present tense (like adding 'is' in English), and changed the ending to make it easier to say
Added و and alif maqsurah at end to make the complete noun form
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start. The middle letter got quiet (sukun). The ى at the end shows it's a special verb that ends in a vowel.
Added تُ prefix to mean 'you' and changed middle vowel to ـِي to make it present tense 'conduct/manage'
Changed from past to present tense by adding يُ at start and doubling the middle letter with ّ
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action. The ا in the middle drops out when we add نَا.
Changes from a verb (to protect) into a noun meaning 'one who protects'. Like changing 'protect' to 'protector'
Added Form VIII pattern (ا and ت) to show mutual action between two parties
Added ة at the end to make it a complete noun meaning 'life'

meaning: they became guided (found the right path themselves)
they are called/invited (referring to a group being called to Allah's Book)
note: Added ون (which becomes وْنَ) to make it plural. The ى changes back to و before the plural ending.
Added وا at the end to show multiple people, and the ى changes because of this
Changed ا to ىٰ because it's a weak letter word
Changed to present tense form with 'you' as the doer

Changed from 'he sought refuge' to 'I seek refuge' in present tense. The middle letter changes to ي because it's a hollow verb.
The و changes to ي and gets doubled (shadda) to show this is someone who leads a lot or has authority
Added اِ at start and ط after ص to show doing something for a special purpose. The و at the end changed to ى because it's at the end of the word.

Added أ at the beginning to show this is the most/closest form. The weak letter ي changes to ى at the end. This word connects to إِبْرٰهِيمَ (Ibrahim) in the ayah to show who people are closest to.
The أ changes to ؤ after يُ, and ى is written with an alif above it (ىٰ) at the end
Changed to 'was given' form. The middle letter ت changed to و because it's a special type of verb. Like saying 'I give' → 'I was given'
The ي at the beginning shows it's happening now or in the future. This is a verb, not a noun, so it doesn't have case endings.
Added يُ prefix to show present tense. The initial أ changes to ؤ after يُ. Changed middle vowel to kasra for present tense form.
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. This shows an ongoing action of twisting/distorting that happens alongside their saying (يَقُولُونَ) in the verse.

meaning: he was able (to do حِجُّ ٱلْبَيْتِ)
note: Added است at the beginning to show seeking or trying to do something. The و changed to ا because it came between two vowels. This word relates to being able to perform the Hajj mentioned in the ayah.
Added ت prefix for 'you' and changed vowels to show present tense. The ى changes to ي in present tense due to weak letter rules.

Added تُ at start to show 'you' and changed vowels to show present tense. The و changed to ي because it's between two vowels
Changed from basic 'protection' to a stronger form meaning 'careful guarding' by adding ت at start and end
Changed from 'he was' to 'you were' by adding تُ at the end. The و in the middle drops out because it's between two vowels.
The weak letter و at the end changes to ا. This word is connected to حُفْرَةٍ (pit) in the ayah, showing being on the edge of a pit.
Added يَ at the beginning to show the action happens in the present/future. The middle letter gets a sukun (ْ) in this form.
The root letters changed to make 'he returned' - the middle letter و turned into ا, which is common for hollow verbs

meaning: they disobeyed (connected to يَكْفُرُونَ - they disbelieve)
note: Added وا at the end to show many people did this action. The ي changes to و because it's a weak letter at the end when we add وا. This word is connected to يَكْفُرُونَ (they disbelieve) in the ayah, showing multiple actions they did.
Changes to present tense by adding يَ at start. The middle letter gets sukun (ْ) and the last vowel changes to damma (ُ). The final و shows it's ongoing action.
Changed the root letters to make a word for things that are closer to us (like this life compared to the afterlife)
Added ت to show the subject is feminine (البَغْضَآءُ meaning 'hatred'). The final ا is dropped when ت is added.

The weak letter ي changes to و because it's at the end of a plural past tense verb
Added وْا۟ at the end to show multiple people did the action. The و returns because it's followed by another letter.

meaning: one who goes around (doer of همَّت)
note: The word changed from a past tense verb 'went around' to a noun meaning 'one who goes around' by adding an ا after the first letter and an ِ after the second letter. This person/thing is doing the action in the sentence.
Added 'wa' (and) at start, 'ta' shows 'you', doubled middle letter shows protecting yourself
Changed alif to madda for pronunciation
Added أ at beginning to show comparison. The ا at the end changes to ى in this form.
Added ون at the end to make it plural. The ى changes back to و before this ending.

meaning: so He rewarded you all (with غَمًّا)
note: Adding كُمْ shows who received the reward. The alif is written as ٰ because it comes before كُمْ.

Changed to plural form by: 1) changing يَ to يُ at start, 2) adding ون at end which causes final ي to drop, and 3) changing vowel before ون to match. This word is one of the actions of the group who are speaking (يَقُولُونَ) in this verse.

meaning: they show/reveal (what they don't show to you)

The word 'to be' changes to 'I was' by adding ت at the end and dropping the و in the middle. This is a common pattern when making 'I' forms in the past.
Adding اِتْ changes it to mean meeting with someone/something mutually

meaning: you were gentle (referring to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
note: Changed the verb to show 'you' did the action in the past. The middle letter ا is dropped because it's a weak letter when adding تَ.
Added sukun and damma to make basic command form. The final و drops because it's a weak letter at the end of a command.
The final ي is dropped because this verb comes after a word that makes it conditional (like 'if' or 'whoever')
The doubled middle letter (ّ) makes it mean 'causes purification' instead of just 'purifies'. The يُ at the start shows it's happening in the present. The weak letter و changes to ي at the end.
Changes ending to show it belongs to someone (like 'my brothers' or 'his brothers')
Added وْا at the end to show many people did the action
Changed to command form by dropping the last letter and adding a silent alif at start

Changed to plural form by adding وا and replacing final ى with و. The hamza at the start changed to ٱ due to connection with previous word.

meaning: we give time (to كَفَرُوا - those who disbelieved)
note: Added نُ at start to mean 'we', changed vowels to show present tense, and changed ا to ي at end to connect with كَفَرُوا (those who disbelieved)
Changed و to ي because it's easier to pronounce, but keeps the same meaning of inheritance
Adding أ at the start makes it mean causing the action to happen to someone else
Changed from 'he was disgraced' to 'disgrace!' as a command, making it shorter by removing letters
Changed to noun form by adding alif after second root letter. This makes it describe the thing itself rather than the action.

meaning: they were mindful (of رَبَّهُمْ - their Lord)
note: Added وْا at the end to show many people did this action. Changed initial alif to ٱ for connection with previous word.
Changed to past tense verb with middle alif, common for hollow verbs

meaning: in twos, paired (describing how to marry wives)
note: Added the long alif (ٰ) to show this is the final form. In this ayah, it describes how men can marry up to four wives, but they should marry them two at a time.
Changed to 'I' form by adding ت at the end. The middle letter و got dropped and we added the ِ (i) sound to keep it easy to say - this happens with similar words that have و in the middle.
The root letters ع-و-ل became عَالَ by adding vowels and changing the middle letter و to ا, making it past tense 'he became dependent'
Changed to present tense with ت prefix for 'you'. The middle ا changes to ُ in present tense of hollow verbs.

Present tense verb starting with يُ meaning 'he directs'. The و from root و-ص-ي changes to long vowel و when يُ is added at start.

The و changes to ا and the ه becomes ء at the end. This is a common pattern for words about natural substances.
Added plural ending ون to show multiple people doing the action. This word connects to يُرِيدُونَ (they want) in the ayah, showing the same group is doing both actions.
The root letters got vowels to make it a past tense verb 'he'. The middle letter و changed to ا which often happens with weak letters.
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action, and the ى changes back to يْ before suffixes

Changed to present tense. The يُ shows it's happening now or in the future. This word connects to يَعِدُهُمْ (he promises them) in the verse.

The transformation described is not accurate. 'Ibn' (son) comes from a different root than 'baniy' (built). They are separate words, not a transformation of one to the other. A better note would be: These are two different words that happen to look similar but have different meanings and origins.

meaning: he is able to (subject of يُنَزِّلَ)
note: Added يَسْتَ at the beginning to show trying or seeking ability. The ُ at the end shows this is the subject doing the action. This word connects to يُنَزِّلَ (to send down) in the ayah, showing what he is able to do.
The root letters ش-ي-أ were joined together and given vowel marks to make the common word شَيْء meaning 'a thing'. The hamza appears as ء at the end.

meaning: you/she comes/brings (subject of أَن in أَن تَبْتَغِىَ)
note: The تَ prefix shows this is happening now. This word follows أَن (that) which makes it subjunctive, meaning it's describing a possible action.

meaning: he flies (with بِجَنَاحَيْهِ - with his two wings)

The weak letter و changes to ي and gets doubled with shadda to show something that is intensely or perfectly upright/correct

Added أَ at beginning to show 'I' am doing the action, and ُ on first letter and ْ on second letter to show it's happening now. The و drops out because it's a weak letter.

Changes to present tense 'we' form. In this ayah, this verb shows how Allah bestows His mercy, with بِرَحْمَتِنَا (with Our mercy) showing how the reaching/striking happens.
Added ي at the end to mean 'my father'
The ن at the start shows 'we' are doing the action
Added ا at the end to show this is the object being acted upon. This word is what is being protected in نَحْفَظُ أَخَانَا (we protect our brother).

Added نَّا at the end to show 'we' in the past tense. The و is dropped because it comes between two vowels. This word connects to سٰرِقِينَ (thieves) to mean 'we were thieves'.
The middle letter ا is dropped and vowels change because this is a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter)
Added أَ at beginning to show 'I' am doing the action. Changed middle vowel to damma and removed last vowel because it's a present tense verb. The و at the end is from the root.
Changed spelling to match Quranic orthography. This word comes after لا (not) to mean 'he does not lose hope'

meaning: I say (connected to لَّكُمْ - to you all)

note: Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start. The middle letter gets sukun (ْ) and the last root letter gets kasra (ِ). The ي at the end is written as ى because it's at the end of the word.
Added يُ at start to make it present tense (happening now). The middle letter got a kasra (ِ) to show it's being done to something. The ending changed to ي.
Added تَ prefix to show present tense feminine subject. The middle ا changes back to ي in present tense.
The alif is added after و to show who does the action. This word comes after مِن (from/of) in the ayah.

The middle letter ي changes to ا because it's a weak letter
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing internal vowels

meaning: he gave something valuable to free/save
note: Connected the letters with fathas to make a past tense verb. The ي at the end changes to ى because it's a weak letter at the end.
Added alif superscript (ٰ) after ى which is a spelling convention
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Changed from basic 'good' root to a special form meaning 'extra good/blessed' by adding و and damma mark
Connected the root letters and added vowels. The middle letter ي becomes ا because it's a weak letter.

Changed weak letter و to ا and added tanween damma at end
Root letters combine with long 'aa' sound and special ending to make the noun meaning 'sign'
Changed from 'he' to 'they' by adding ون at the end - like adding 's' in English to make it plural
Changed from past to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing middle letter from و to ي
Added تَ prefix to show present tense feminine subject (referring to الفُلْك - the ship). Changed vowels to show present tense.
Changed final ي to و to show multiple people doing the action instead of one person

meaning: we hide (connected to نُعْلِنُ - we announce)
note: Added نُ at the beginning to show 'we' are doing the action. The ي changes to ى because it's at the end. The middle letter gets a sukun (ْ) to show it's in present tense. This word is paired with نُعْلِنُ (we announce) to contrast hiding versus announcing.

meaning: he/it is hidden (something that happens to اللَّهِ)

note: Added alif khanjariyah (ٰ) to show the long vowel sound more clearly. This doesn't change the meaning.

meaning: we will be responded to (to your call)
note: Added نُ at start to show 'we' as subject, dropped final vowel, and ي dropped between vowels. This is part of the phrase 'we will respond to your call' in the ayah.

Added تَ at start to show present tense. The ا changes to ى at the end in present tense. The middle letter gets sukun.
Changed from past to present by adding يُ at start. The ending changed because it comes after لَمْ in the sentence
Added نا to show 'we' did the action. The ى changes back to يْ before نا.
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did the action. The ى changes to يْ before adding تُ
The و at the start is dropped, and وا is added at the end to make it a command for a group
Added و at the end to show 'we' are doing the rescuing (like saying 'we will save' instead of just 'will save')
Added تُ at start and changed middle letter to make 'you are' instead of 'he did'

Added أ at the beginning to show making someone else rich. The ي changes to ى because it's at the end.
Added تَ at the start to show 'he made himself' do the action, and changed ending to ى because it's a special type of verb that ends in ى
Added ت at start to mean 'you/she' and changed vowels to make it present tense

Changed from past tense 'he counted' to a command 'count!' by changing the vowels. The ي at the end becomes و because it's easier to say

Added vowel marks and the ى at the end because it's a special word ending in ي. This word shows what happened before the ceiling fell (فَخَرَّ).

Changed to a command form by: 1) Adding damma on first letter, 2) Dropping the middle weak letter و, and 3) Making the last letter silent. This word is what Allah says when commanding something to exist, as mentioned in قَوْلُنَا (our word/saying).
